followups.original_subject: Rachel — your Skipton garden design site isn't mobile-friendly
followups.subject: One thing I spotted about Skipton Garden Design
followups.body:
Rachel,

Following up on my note from last week.

I had a look at your Google listing and something jumped out — you've got lovely work in the Google photos but no portfolio section on the website. Garden design is a highly visual sale, and customers want to see what they're getting.

A small gallery section on the site — even 6 or 8 photos with brief project descriptions — would do a lot of work. Happy to chat about whether that's worth adding.

[Your name]
followups.notes: Day 7 new angle. Shift from mobile issue to a new concrete opportunity (no portfolio = missed visual sales tool). Spring timing is apt for garden design.

followups.original_subject: Boroughbridge Plumbing — site not showing in mobile searches
followups.subject: Quick question — Boroughbridge Plumbing
followups.body:
Hi,

Dropping a quick line to follow up from last week.

I had another look — Boroughbridge Plumbing isn't appearing in the Google Maps 3-pack for 'plumber Boroughbridge' searches. The mobile issue is part of it, but there are also a couple of local SEO signals missing from the site that would push you up the results.

Happy to run a free local SEO snapshot and share what I find — takes 10 minutes.

[Your name]
followups.notes: Day 7 new angle. No owner name so keep generic. Shift to local SEO angle — more concrete business outcome than just 'mobile optimisation'.
